market: AI remains an infrastructure-led market: revenue and cash conversion are still cleanest at the compute, networking, and capacity-owner layers. The market is starting to separate productive AI capex from narrative-heavy spend.
tech: Enterprise adoption is real and deepening, especially in coding, research, and structured workflows. Broad autonomous agents are still narrower in practice than the marketing suggests.
risk: Top risks are capex outrunning monetization, power and deployment bottlenecks limiting usable capacity, model-layer commoditization, and export-control tightening reshaping global demand maps.
